question: What was the score for Norman's Ice Cream and Freezes on 2014-09-08? Norman's Ice Cream and Freezes refers to name = 'Norman's Ice Cream and Freezes'; on 2014-09-08 refers to date = '2014-09-08 00:00:00'
orig_question: What was the potiential for Francesco Parravicini on 2010/8/30? Francesco Parravicini refers to player_name = 'Francesco Parravicini'; on 2010/8/30 refers to date = '2010-08-30 00:00:00'
orig_masked: What was the [C158] for [V1] on [V3]? [V1] refers to [C126] = '[V1]'; on [V3] refers to [C136] = '[V2]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C126]; [V2] is a value of the column [C136]; [V3] is a value of the column [C136]
evidence: Norman's Ice Cream and Freezes refers to name = 'Norman's Ice Cream and Freezes'; on 2014-09-08 refers to date = '2014-09-08 00:00:00'
orig_evidence: Francesco Parravicini refers to player_name = 'Francesco Parravicini'; on 2010/8/30 refers to date = '2010-08-30 00:00:00'
gold_sql: SELECT T1.score FROM inspections AS T1 INNER JOIN businesses AS T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id WHERE T2.name = 'Norman''s Ice Cream and Freezes' AND T1.date = '2014-09-08'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT t2.potential FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id WHERE SUBSTR(t2.`date`, 1, 10) = '2010-08-30' AND t1.player_name = 'Francesco Parravicini'

question: List down at least five full names of competitors with Canada region name. Canada region name refers to noc_region.region_name = 'Canada' WHERE person_region.region_id = noc_region.id; Name of competitors refers to person.full_name;
orig_question: List down at least five full names of superheroes with blue eyes. blue eyes refers to colour.colour = 'Blue' WHERE eye_colour_id = colour.id; Name of superheroes refers to superhero_name;
orig_masked: List down at least five [C28] of [T9] with [V1] [C19]. [V1] [C19] refers to [C5].[C5] = '[V1]' WHERE [C19] = [C5].[C6]; [C28] of [T9] refers to [C28];; [V1] is a value of the column [C5]
evidence: Canada region name refers to noc_region.region_name = 'Canada' WHERE person_region.region_id = noc_region.id; Name of competitors refers to person.full_name;
orig_evidence: blue eyes refers to colour.colour = 'Blue' WHERE eye_colour_id = colour.id; Name of superheroes refers to superhero_name;
gold_sql: SELECT full_name FROM person AS T1 INNER JOIN person_region AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.person_id INNER JOIN noc_region AS T3 ON T2.region_id = T3.id WHERE T3.region_name = 'Canada' LIMIT 5
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.superhero_name FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN colour AS T2 ON T1.eye_colour_id = T2.id WHERE T2.colour = 'Blue' LIMIT 5
note: REVIEWED: Fixed colour.colour and colour.id link

question: What are the names of the games with the genre name of Puzzle?  name of games refers to refers to game_name; the genre name of Puzzle refers to genre_name = 'Puzzle'
orig_question: What are the names of the superheroes with the power of death touch?  name of superheroes refers to refers to superhero_name; the power of death touch refers to power_name = 'Death Touch'
orig_masked: What are the [C28] of the [T9] with the [C31] of [V1]?  [C28] refers to refers to [C28]; the [C31] of [V1] refers to [C31] = '[V1]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C31]
evidence: name of games refers to refers to game_name; the genre name of Puzzle refers to genre_name = 'Puzzle'
orig_evidence: name of superheroes refers to refers to superhero_name; the power of death touch refers to power_name = 'Death Touch'
gold_sql: SELECT T1.game_name FROM game as T1 INNER JOIN genre AS T2 ON T1.genre_id = T2.id WHERE genre_name = 'Puzzle'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.superhero_name FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN hero_power AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.hero_id INNER JOIN superpower AS T3 ON T2.power_id = T3.id WHERE T3.power_name = 'Death Touch'

question: How many patients treated with Cesarean section have a normal age? treated with Cesarean section refers to procedures.description = 'Cesarean section'; normal age refers to birthdate between 1980 and 2000;
orig_question: How many patients diagnosed with SLE have a normal white blood cell level? diagnosed with SLE refers to Diagnosis = 'SLE'; normal white blood cell level refers to WBC between 3.5 and 9.0;
orig_masked: How many [T3] [C61] with [V1] have a [C57]? [C61] with [V1] refers to [C61] = '[V1]'; [C57] refers to [C57] between [V2] and [V3].0;; [V1] is a value of the column [C61]; [V2] is a value of the column [C57]; [V3] is a value of the column [C57]
evidence: treated with Cesarean section refers to procedures.description = 'Cesarean section'; normal age refers to birthdate between 1980 and 2000;
orig_evidence: diagnosed with SLE refers to Diagnosis = 'SLE'; normal white blood cell level refers to WBC between 3.5 and 9.0;
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.patient) FROM patients AS T1 INNER JOIN procedures AS T2 ON T1.patient = T2.patient WHERE T2.description = 'Cesarean section' AND STRFTIME('%Y', T1.birthdate) BETWEEN '1980' AND '2000'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.Diagnosis = 'SLE' AND T2.WBC BETWEEN 3.5 AND 9
note: REVIEWED: Fixed the value like to grab the full string rep

question: Of the vehicles with an normal level of horsepower, how many of them produced in Asia? normal level of horsepower refers to 100 < horsepower < 200; produced in Asia refers to country.country = 'Japan';
orig_question: Of the patients with an normal level of IGG, how many of them admitted to the hospital? normal level of IGG refers to 900 < IGG < 2000; admitted to the hospital refers to Admission = '+';
orig_masked: Of the [T3] with an [C34], how many of them [C58]? [C34] refers to [V2] < IGG < [V1]; [C58] refers to [C58] = '[V3]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C34]; [V2] is a value of the column [C34]; [V3] is a value of the column [C58]
evidence: normal level of horsepower refers to 100 < horsepower < 200; produced in Asia refers to country.country = 'Japan';
orig_evidence: normal level of IGG refers to 900 < IGG < 2000; admitted to the hospital refers to Admission = '+';
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ID) FROM data as T1 INNER JOIN production AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID INNER JOIN country AS T3 ON T2.country = T3.origin WHERE T3.country = 'Japan' AND T1.horsepower > 100 AND T1.horsepower < 200
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.IGG > 900 AND T2.IGG <2000 AND  T1.Admission = '+'
note: REVIEWED: Fixed missing link to IGG

question: Which movies had an length of over 150 from 2008 to 2010? Please list titles. runtime > 150; from 2008 to 2010 refers to strftime('%Y', Release Date) BETWEEN '2008' AND '2010';
orig_question: Which players had an overall rating of over 80 from 2008 to 2010? Please list player names. overall_rating > 80; from 2008 to 2010 refers to strftime('%Y', date) BETWEEN '2008' AND '2010';
orig_masked: Which [T4] had an [C153] of over [V3] from [V1] to [V2]? Please list [T4] [C126]. [C153] > [V3]; from [V1] to [V2] refers to strftime('%Y', [C136]) BETWEEN '[V1]' AND '[V2]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C136]; [V2] is a value of the column [C136]; [V3] is a value of the column [C153]
evidence: runtime > 150; from 2008 to 2010 refers to strftime('%Y', Release Date) BETWEEN '2008' AND '2010';
orig_evidence: overall_rating > 80; from 2008 to 2010 refers to strftime('%Y', date) BETWEEN '2008' AND '2010';
gold_sql: SELECT title FROM movie WHERE runtime > 150 AND strftime('%Y', "Release Date") BETWEEN '2008' AND '2010';
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT t1.player_name FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id WHERE t2.overall_rating > 80 AND SUBSTR(t2.`date`, 1, 4) BETWEEN '2008' AND '2010'
note: REVIEWED: Fixed with a more specific link

question: How many male competitors have their height in the normal range? male refers to gender = 'M'; height in the normal range refers to height < 200;
orig_question: How many male patients have their glutamic oxaloacetic transaminase in the normal range? male refers to Sex = 'M'; glutamic oxaloacetic transaminase in the normal range refers to GOT < 60;
orig_masked: How many [C64] [T3] have their [C28] in the [C28]? [C64] refers to [C64] = '[V2]'; [C28] in the [C28] refers to [C28] < [V1];; [V1] is a value of the column [C28]; [V2] is a value of the column [C64]
evidence: male refers to gender = 'M'; height in the normal range refers to height < 200;
orig_evidence: male refers to Sex = 'M'; glutamic oxaloacetic transaminase in the normal range refers to GOT < 60;
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT id) FROM person WHERE height < 200 AND gender = 'M'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.GOT < 60 AND T1.SEX = 'M'

question: Please provide the names of top three competitors who are over 200 cm tall in alphabetical order. over 200 cm tall refers to height > 200; name of competitor refers to full_name
orig_question: Please provide the names of top three football players who are over 180 cm tall in alphabetical order. over 180 cm tall refers to height > 180; name of football player refers to player_name
orig_masked: Please provide the [C126] of top three [T4] who are over [V1] cm [C122] in alphabetical order. over [V1] cm [C122] refers to [C122] > [V1]; [C126] of [T4] refers to [C126]; [V1] is a value of the column [C122]
evidence: over 200 cm tall refers to height > 200; name of competitor refers to full_name
orig_evidence: over 180 cm tall refers to height > 180; name of football player refers to player_name
gold_sql: SELECT full_name FROM person WHERE height > 200 ORDER BY full_name ASC LIMIT 3
orig_gold_sql: SELECT player_name FROM Player WHERE height > 180 ORDER BY player_name LIMIT 3

question: How many authors who choose California are staying in Oakland city? authors.city contains the data of city; 'CA' represents for 'California'.
orig_question: How many accounts who choose issuance after transaction are staying in East Bohemia region? A3 contains the data of region; 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU' represents for 'issuance after transaction'.
orig_masked: How many [T1] who choose [C4] are [C25] in [V2] [C25]? [C25] contains the data of [C25]; '[V1]' represents for '[C4]'.; [V1] is a value of the column [C4]; [V2] is a value of the column [C25]
evidence: authors.city contains the data of city; 'CA' represents for 'California'.
orig_evidence: A3 contains the data of region; 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU' represents for 'issuance after transaction'.
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT au_id) FROM authors WHERE state = 'CA' AND city = 'Oakland'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T2.account_id) FROM district AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.A3 = 'east Bohemia' AND T2.frequency = 'POPLATEK PO OBRATU'

question: How many PG-rated comedies are there in the films?  PG-rated refers to film.rating = 'PG' WHERE film_category.category_id = category.category_id; comedies refers to category.name = 'Comedy';
orig_question: How many green-skinned villains are there in the superhero universe?  green-skinned refers to colour.colour = 'Green' WHERE skin_colour_id = colour.id; villains refers to alignment = 'Bad';
orig_masked: How many [V1]-[C27] [C1] are there in the [T9]?  [V1]-[C27] refers to [C5] = '[V1]' WHERE [C27] = [C6]; [C1] refers to [C1] = '[V2]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C5]; [V2] is a value of the column [C1]
evidence: PG-rated refers to film.rating = 'PG' WHERE film_category.category_id = category.category_id; comedies refers to category.name = 'Comedy';
orig_evidence: green-skinned refers to colour.colour = 'Green' WHERE skin_colour_id = colour.id; villains refers to alignment = 'Bad';
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.film_id) FROM film AS T1 INNER JOIN film_category AS T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id INNER JOIN category AS T3 ON T2.category_id = T3.category_id WHERE T3.name = 'Comedy' AND T1.rating = 'PG'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.id) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN alignment AS T2 ON T1.alignment_id = T2.id INNER JOIN colour AS T3 ON T1.skin_colour_id = T3.id WHERE T2.alignment = 'Bad' AND T3.colour = 'Green'
